$comment: docs/specs/hermes-tool-manifest.json — The enforceable form of 'Hermes is REST-only'. Hermes's tool-dispatch layer loads exactly this file; a tool call not listed here is rejected before any HTTP request is made. Every endpoint maps 1:1 to docs/specs/api-contract.yaml. Deliberately absent: anything that creates/modifies rules or schedules (no n8n endpoints, no webhook registration, no scene *creation* — Hermes executes scenes, humans define them), all auth/user management, device create/delete, and all MQTT (Hermes has no broker credentials by construction).

auth.description: Long-lived service JWT with claim actor_type=hermes; backend writes audit_log.actor_type from this claim and audit_log.actor_context from the X-Hermes-Context header ({conversation_id, message_id}) Hermes sends on every state-changing call.

tools.name: list_rooms
tools.description: List all rooms with device counts. Use to resolve room names the user mentions.

tools.name: list_devices
tools.description: List devices, optionally filtered by room, type, or online status. Use to resolve which device the user means before reading or writing.

tools.name: get_device
tools.description: Get one device with all its capabilities and their current values.

tools.name: read_capability
tools.description: Read the current value of one capability on one device (e.g. temperature in the bedroom).

tools.name: read_capability_history
tools.description: Read historical readings for a capability over a time range, optionally downsampled. Use for questions like 'how warm was it last night'.

tools.name: write_capability
tools.description: Write a value to a writable capability (POST /devices/{id}/capabilities/{capability}). Covers simple actuators: power (bool), ir_command (enum). For ac_control use write_ac_control instead.

value.description: bool for power; enum string from the capability's config.values for ir_command

tools.name: write_ac_control
tools.description: Set air-conditioning state (mode, setpoint, fan) via the ac_control capability. Higher-impact than a light toggle (comfort + energy), so requires user confirmation.

tools.name: list_scenes
tools.description: List available scenes and their actions so the user can be told what a scene will do before running it.

tools.name: execute_scene
tools.description: Execute an existing scene by id. Hermes may run scenes but can never create, modify, or schedule them — scenes are defined by humans, schedules/triggers live in n8n.

tools.name: get_system_metrics
tools.description: Host CPU/RAM/disk/uptime numbers for questions like 'how's the server doing'.

tools.name: get_weather
tools.description: Cached weather (current + short forecast) from the backend's weather passthrough. Never call an external weather provider directly.

tools.name: query_audit_log
tools.description: Query recent audit entries, e.g. to answer 'who turned off the AC'. Read-only.

tools.name: save_conversation_turn
tools.description: Internal: persist user/assistant/tool messages to hermes_conversations / hermes_messages via the backend. Not exposed to the LLM as a callable tool — listed here because the manifest is the complete allowlist of Hermes's outbound endpoints.

tools.name: report_ai_usage
tools.description: Internal: write one ai_usage_log row per OpenRouter call (model, tokens, cost, latency, conversation/message link). Not exposed to the LLM.

denied_by_design: POST/PATCH/DELETE /devices — device lifecycle is manual, dashboard-only
POST/PATCH/DELETE /scenes — Hermes executes scenes, never defines them
Any n8n URL — Hermes cannot create, modify, trigger-register, or invoke workflows; 'when X then Y' is out of Hermes's power entirely
Any MQTT operation — Hermes holds no broker credentials
$note: The two internal:true entries require a small additive backend surface (POST message persistence + POST /ai/usage) not in Phase A's read-focused contract — flagged as an addendum to api-contract.yaml rather than silently assumed.
